content:
Unlike massive Ontario or BC granting agencies where you just upload a PDF to an anonymous portal, ACOA operates on relationships. Your assigned ACOA program officer will literally champion your application to the committee. Call them BEFORE you start writing your application to align your project with their current quarterly budget priorities.

shortAnswer: Yes — Atlantic Canada small businesses can access ACOA interest-free loans (via BDP and REGI), Nova Scotia productivity vouchers, and New Brunswick Opportunities NB payroll subsidies. These stack alongside federal programs like IRAP and SR&ED.
shortAnswerQuestion: What small business grants and funding are available in Atlantic Canada for 2026?

faq.question: Are ACOA BDP loans interest-free?
faq.answer: Yes. BDP loans are interest-free and feature flexible repayment schedules tied to project completion milestones, making them highly patient debt sources.

faq.question: Are retail stores eligible for ACOA?
faq.answer: Generally, no. ACOA prioritizes businesses that bring new export revenues or critical infrastructure into the Atlantic region. Local services are usually excluded unless they target tourism.

faq.question: Can I combine ACOA with federal IRAP?
faq.answer: Yes, you can stack them. However, total government funding across all levels (municipal, provincial, and federal) typically cannot exceed 75% to 85% of total eligible project costs.

expertTip.title: Engage Your Local Officer Early
expertTip.content: ACOA relies heavily on local regional officers. Contacting your local office and obtaining feedback on a project concept before submitting a formal proposal increases approval success rates by 35%.
